description: Baseline for chimp128 runtime using just bench-target; add autoresearch.md/autoresearch.sh/autoresearch.checks.sh infrastructure
Optimize chimp128 encode/decode ring-buffer indexing: replace iterator-based fallback scan with manual bounded loop and use bitmask (&127) for modulo-128 indexing
Reduce chimp128 lookup overhead: use u32 lookup table in encode and remove unused decode lookup table writes
Speed up bin helpers used by chimp128 by replacing tiny-array iteration/position lookups with direct match-based bin_count/bin_encode/bin_decode
Tested forcing inline(always) on chimp bin helper functions; benchmark regressed so reverted
Tried switching decode ring-buffer safety assert! to debug_assert! to remove runtime check overhead; no improvement over best
Replace chimp128 encode fallback ring-buffer scan with O(1) previous-value reference when hash lookup misses, removing per-sample 128-entry search
Attempted removing encode lookup table entirely and always using previous ring entry reference; faster but broke chimp128 golden vector encoding compatibility
Minor hot-loop cleanup: iterate encode input as `for &curr in &input[1..]` to avoid repeated dereferencing, plus retain updated autoresearch notes
Tried preallocating decode output with Vec::with_capacity(count); benchmark regressed on target workload
Tested zero-initializing chimp128 encode ring buffer now that lookup-miss path no longer scans sentinels; no improvement
Optimize bitstream hot paths: add byte-aligned fast paths in put_u64_lowest_bits/read_u64_lowest_bits and inline bit operations in src/bits.rs
Reduce chimp128 encode leading-zero overhead by using new bin_leading_and_code(xor) helper to compute bin count and 3-bit code in one match
Tried wrapping_sub-based lookup age check in chimp128 encode; compile failed due ambiguous integer type for index
Retried chimp128 lookup age check with wrapping_sub and explicit usize index; benchmark near best but correctness checks failed (gorilla property test), so discarded
Fix gorilla correctness for high-leading-zero xors by capping encoded leading-zero count to 31 (5-bit field); checks now stable and chimp128 benchmark also improved
Retested replacing chimp128 decode assert! with debug_assert! after bitstream optimizations; still slightly worse than best
Tried reusing a precomputed chimp128 lookup key (`(curr_bits as usize) & 0x3FFF`) to avoid duplicate mask/cast; regressed
Add dedicated aligned 64-bit fast paths in bit I/O: use put_u64 for writes and from_be_bytes slice read for count==64 when bit-aligned
Tried unsafe unaligned u64 read in aligned count==64 Bitread fast path to avoid byte copy; regressed benchmark
Retested preallocating chimp128 decode output Vec with capacity(count); still slower on target benchmark
Tried table-based bin_decode array indexing instead of match; slight regression vs current best
Removed chimp128 decode ring-buffer initialization assert check entirely; no improvement versus best
Tried preallocating chimp128 encode BytesMut with input.len()*8 capacity to avoid growth reallocations; no gain
Tested forcing inline(always) for hot bitstream methods after adding aligned fast paths; no further improvement
Tried 0-sentinel lookup encoding (store index+1) in chimp128 encode to avoid u32::MAX checks; significantly slower
Tried index-based while loop for chimp128 encode input traversal instead of slice iterator; regressed
Tried while-loop traversal in chimp128 decode to reduce iterator overhead; slightly slower than best
Re-tested chimp128 encode lookup table as usize (vs u32) after other optimizations; clearly slower
Tried generic byte-multiple fast paths (count%8==0) in bit read/write using to_be_bytes/copy_from_slice; significantly slower
Tested forcing inline(always) for bin_leading_and_code helper used in chimp128 encode; regressed
Tried omitting trailing zero flush byte when bitstream ends on byte boundary in Bitwrite::into_bytes; breaks golden encoded byte compatibility across codecs
Retested zero-initializing chimp128 encode ring buffer with current optimized bit paths; regressed heavily
Tried using slice try_into for aligned 64-bit Bitread fast path instead of copy_from_slice; slower
